title: Ranking lib + rank CLI: cluster, rank, and defuse the datamark trap at the comparison key
action: Implement the slice B ranking engine in lib/feedback.mjs per plan.md revision 3 ('Ranking, defined against the real corpus'): (1) normalizeTitle(title) — repeatedly strip the datamark wrapper (U+00AB...U+00BB guillemets) until fixed-point (this defuses BOTH the datamark asymmetry trap — foreign titles stored wrapped, local bare — and datamark's double-wrap non-idempotence), then casefold and collapse whitespace. Stored entries stay wrapped: D2b's mergeDigests behavior is untouched; only the comparison key is stripped. (2) clusterEntries(mergedView) — group entries by normalizeTitle key; each cluster carries {key, entries, pain, frequency, corroboration}: pain = max entry pain, frequency = cluster size, corroboration = count of DISTINCT repos contributing (local counts as one). (3) rankClusters(clusters) — rank = pain * frequency * corroboration, sorted descending; deterministic tie-break: earliest first_seen ascending, then key lexicographic; output over a pinned digest is byte-identical (same idempotence discipline as buildDigest). (4) A thin 'rank' subcommand in bee_feedback.mjs (parseArgs/run/main shape like the existing subcommands, --json support, no business logic in the CLI). (5) Tests for every slice B matrix row in plan.md. Sync both vendored copies in .bee/bin/ byte-identical to the templates.

truths: a foreign wrapped title and an identical bare local title land in ONE cluster of 2 (the trap test)
a double-wrapped title also unifies into the same cluster (fixed-point strip)
corroboration is 2 when local + one synthetic foreign repo share a cluster key, 1 when disjoint
rankClusters over a pinned digest is byte-identical across two runs; tie-break exercised by equal-rank fixtures
empty digest yields [] without throwing; distinct titles never falsely unify

artifacts.substantive: normalizeTitle + clusterEntries + rankClusters, pure functions over the merged view
rank subcommand wired to the lib
slice B matrix rows: trap, double-wrap, corroboration, determinism, empty, non-cluster

key_links: normalizeTitle strips at the comparison key only — mergeDigests' stored datamark wrapping (D2b) is untouched
rank subcommand consumes mergeDigests' merged view, never a raw foreign digest
prohibitions: Never modify mergeDigests' datamark or revalidation behavior — the trap is defused at the comparison key, not by reopening the merge contract
Never weaken, skip, or delete any of the 110 existing assertions
No non-deterministic input (clock, LLM, fs order) reaches rank computation
No C0 control character as a data separator in vendored source
